title: Make Metadata Runtime Access Explicit
prompt: Use $nextjs-cache-components-runtime to improve this code according to cache-metadata-runtime.
notes: Metadata and viewport runtime access is tracked separately from the page. Cache the metadata source or explicitly defer to request time.

title: Replace Route Segment Cache Flags with Component Boundaries
prompt: Use $nextjs-cache-components-runtime to improve this code according to cache-migration-segment-configs.
notes: When Cache Components are enabled, replace route-level dynamic/static flags with local cache, Suspense, or request-time boundaries.

title: Use Remote Cache for Durable Shared Runtime Cache
prompt: Use $nextjs-cache-components-runtime to improve this code according to cache-remote-cache.
notes: Use remote cache or a platform cache handler when cache entries must survive serverless cold starts or be shared across instances.

title: Wrap Runtime Data Access in Suspense
prompt: Use $nextjs-cache-components-runtime to improve this code according to cache-runtime-suspense.
notes: With Cache Components, runtime APIs require request context. Use Suspense so the static shell can prerender and the runtime island can stream.

title: Match Invalidation API to User Freshness Needs
prompt: Use $nextjs-cache-components-runtime to improve this code according to cache-tag-invalidation.
notes: Use `updateTag` inside Server Actions when the mutating user should immediately see the new data. Use `revalidateTag` from Route Handlers or when eventual freshness is acceptable.

title: Put `use cache` at the Narrowest Stable Boundary
prompt: Use $nextjs-cache-components-runtime to improve this code according to cache-use-cache-scope.
notes: Apply `use cache` at the narrowest stable boundary. Avoid page-level caching when the page mixes shared and personalized data.
